rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE HOM FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Response To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Continued to be on the 2:00 p.m. law & motion calendar. Petitioner Glenn Hom's Motion to Compel is granted in part and denied in part. The Court sees no reason to reconsider the March 15, 2018 Order ("the prior Order") or to go beyond the statutory verification protections to the extent Respondents Brian and Anna Hom ("Respondents") have verified responses that they have no responsive information. Nor is it appropriate for the Court to reiterate what it has already ordered. On the other hand, the Court is not satisfied that Respondents have demonstrated diligence in obtaining all account records within their custody or control needed to respond completely as required by the prior Order. Within 30 days of the date of this order, Respondents are ordered to (1) obtain copies of the "missing" records for Anna Hom's account(s) (and for Brian Hom's accounts if there are any "missing" records as to his accounts) and review those records for responsive information and documents under the parameters set forth in the prior Order, and supplement their responses accordingly; (2) disclose the last four digits of any account originally held by Lester Hom, to which Brian and/or Anna became a successor in interest ("Lester Accounts"), regardless of whether Respondents have actual statements for those accounts currently in their possession; (3) make a diligent attempt to obtain records for any and all Lester Accounts, and supplement their responses accordingly; and (4) produce the deposit information that corresponds to the checks identified in Special Interrogatories 10 and 12 (regarding checks made out to Brian Hom in the amounts of $10,000 and $26,000). All other relief sought in Petitioner's motion is denied. No sanctions are awarded. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, September 04, 2018.
